rationale.D1: CTRE TunerConstants-generated swerve provides a vendored drivetrain abstraction (D1=2), but all mechanism subsystems (Turret, Transport) hold motor objects directly with no IO interface — there are zero `interface *IO` definitions in the repo, confirming no IO layer exists for mechanisms.
rationale.D2: Button bindings in RobotContainer and command sequences qualify for D1=1 command-based baseline; the Autonomous class is a large static utility with switch/case state machines, but there is no centralized coordinator object with a goal-request API or guarded transitions — it is imperative per-method switching, not a Superstructure.
rationale.D3: CommandSwerveDrivetrain has a sim notifier thread (startSimThread with updateSimState) and Vision.java has PhotonCameraSim/VisionSystemSim wired — token/partial sim exists; but Robot.simulationPeriodic() is empty and no mechanism physics (ElevatorSim etc.) are present, so this is D1/stub-level sim only.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists beyond the build.gradle JUnit dependency declaration; no test files or @Test annotations found anywhere — confirmed D4=0.
rationale.D5: Telemetry.java publishes structured NetworkTables data (StructPublisher for Pose, ChassisSpeeds, module states) and CTRE SignalLogger is started — this is organized NetworkTables publishing at D1/low-D2 level; no AdvantageKit, DogLog, or Epilogue; also heavy System.out.println debugging throughout Robot.java/Autonomous.java puts ceiling at D5=1.
rationale.D6: PathPlanner AutoBuilder is fully configured in CommandSwerveDrivetrain and path files exist, but Robot.autonomousPeriodic() explicitly comments out `m_autonomousCommand.schedule()` and routes all autos to custom Autonomous.* static methods using a home-grown AutoDrive spline system — PathPlanner is present but not used for competition autos; the active system is custom encoder/pose-based, which is D1 (closed-loop with position feedback but not trajectory following).
rationale.D7: Two PhotonVision cameras feed addVisionMeasurement via estConsumer lambda (Robot.java:49-50); Vision.java implements multi-tag vs single-tag std-dev heuristic with dynamic distance-based scaling — confirmed fused, filtered estimation at D3; no TimeInterpolatableBuffer and RobotState is a plain DTO (not a world-model class), so D4 is not reached.
rationale.D8: No README beyond WPILib-License.md, no CONTRIBUTING, no spotless/checkstyle, no .github/workflows — only consistent package structure and a Constants file qualify for minimal D1 hygiene; has_library_repo=true in the manifest counts toward D8 but the season repo itself shows no onboarding or CI artifacts.
